extension.valueString: Lower cutoffs reflect earlier metabolic risk, not a different disease; do not restate as 'South Asians are unhealthy at normal weight'.
WHO Expert Consultation — appropriate BMI for Asian populations (Lancet 2004)
A screening trigger is not a diagnosis; a positive trigger means 'test glucose', not 'has diabetes'.
Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2024
Optimum BMI cut points to screen Asian Americans for type 2 diabetes (Araneta/Kanaya, Diabetes Care 2015)
IDF consensus worldwide definition of the metabolic syndrome — ethnic-specific waist cutoffs
IDF consensus worldwide definition of the metabolic syndrome
WtHR >=0.5 is a general adiposity rule; the South-Asian-specific claim is that it outperforms BMI here, not a distinct cutoff.
Comparison of obesity indices for CVD risk classification in South Asian adults — The CARRS Study (Patel et al., PLoS One 2017)
Prevention of ASCVD in South Asians — NLA clinical perspective (2021)
Male cut equals the general threshold; the SA-specific piece is the female cut and the pattern's weight, not this value.
A normal LDL-C does not rule out risk when TG is high and HDL low; do not present LDL-C as the sole lipid target for SA users.
AHA Scientific Statement — ASCVD in South Asians (Volgman 2018)
Lipid Association of India SA lipid targets (Enas 2013)
Risk factors for early MI in South Asians (INTERHEART, JAMA 2007)
The range is not SA-specific; the SA-specific claim is preference of ApoB over LDL-C, not a different cutoff.
NLA — Prevention of ASCVD in South Asians (2021)

Do NOT claim South Asians universally run high Lp(a): MASALA (Huffman 2019) found no Lp(a)-atherosclerosis signal within the cohort. The rationale is 'genetically fixed + test-once is cheap', plus higher elevated-prevalence, not a proven SA outcome association.
NLA 2024 Lp(a) focused update / AHA 2024 Lp(a) toolkit
LAI targets are risk-stratified; do not present a single non-HDL cutoff as applying to all SA users.
Lipid Association of India SA lipid targets (Enas 2013)
HbA1c is not universally 'wrong' in SA users; the claim is that it can under- or over-read (iron deficiency, glycation rate), so borderline values warrant glucose confirmation.
Isolated HbA1c identifies a different T2D subgroup vs glucose in Asian Indians — CARRS & MASALA (Gujral 2019)

code.text: Coronary artery calcium — baseline discussion age

extension.valueString: AHA Scientific Statement — ASCVD in South Asians (Volgman 2018)
The reference range is not SA-specific; the SA-relevant point is high deficiency prevalence, warranting screening — not a different normal range.
Endocrine Society — Evaluation, Treatment, and Prevention of Vitamin D Deficiency (2011)
Explicitly NOT guideline-backed; present as an optional/exploratory marker, never as recommended screening.
MASALA cardiometabolic profile / HOMA-IR vs MESA (Kanaya 2014)

Do NOT ship an ancestry-adjusted eGFR. The 2021 refit removed the race term as a social-not-biological construct that delayed care. This row exists to instruct consumers to NOT stratify eGFR by ancestry.
New Creatinine- and Cystatin C–Based Equations without Race (CKD-EPI 2021, Inker et al., NEJM)
There is no validated SA multiplier in PREVENT; do not invent one. State the limitation ('treat as a floor'), do not apply a made-up adjustment.
Development and Validation of the AHA PREVENT Equations (Circulation 2024)
The 175 mg/dL cut is not SA-specific; the SA-relevant point is that atherogenic dyslipidemia (high TG + low HDL) reaches it often even at a normal LDL-C.
The >=2 mg/L cut is not SA-specific; SA users more often exceed it due to higher visceral adiposity at the same BMI.
A risk-enhancer is not a risk multiplier; it does not by itself diagnose disease or mandate a statin — it shifts the borderline decision.
LAI targets are risk-stratified; do not present a single cutoff as applying to all SA users. LDL-C <70 matches general very-high-risk guidance; the SA-specific piece is the tighter non-HDL target.
Lipid Association of India SA lipid targets (Enas 2013)
NLA — Prevention of ASCVD in South Asians (2021)
A lower screening BMI reflects earlier ectopic-fat accumulation, not a different disease; MASLD is diagnosed by imaging/enzymes, not by BMI alone.
Multi-society Delphi consensus statement on new fatty liver disease nomenclature (MASLD) (Rinella et al., 2023)
Ectopic fat depots and coronary artery calcium in South Asians vs other ethnic groups (Garg et al., JAHA 2016)
The multiplier applies to SCORE2 specifically (derived from QRISK3 data); do not transplant it onto PREVENT or the Pooled Cohort Equations, which have no validated SA multiplier.
2021 ESC Guidelines on cardiovascular disease prevention in clinical practice

code.text: Coronary artery calcium (South Asian age/sex percentiles)

extension.valueString: These are cohort percentiles (MASALA+DILWALE), not a guideline threshold; CAC=0 remains reassuring at age 50 (55% of men, 80% of women) but less so at 70.
South Asian coronary artery calcium age/sex percentiles — MASALA + DILWALE (Tasdighi et al., JACC Advances 2025)
Explicitly not a guideline cutoff; the top MASALA quartile (>=~33 mg/dL) was associated with incident CAC, not CAC progression. Present as exploratory. Calculate as total cholesterol - HDL-C - LDL-C.
Remnant cholesterol and incident coronary artery calcium in South Asians — MASALA (Manghis et al., J Clin Lipidol 2025)
This row exists to instruct consumers NOT to use APOL1 in a South Asian risk model; including it would be a false-ancestry-attribution error.
APOL1 kidney-risk variants are West-African ancestry–specific (review)
This row exists to instruct consumers NOT to apply East-Asian alcohol-metabolism genetics to South Asians.
ALDH2*2 / ADH1B*2 alcohol-metabolism variants are predominantly East Asian (review)
The reference range is not SA-specific; the SA-relevant point is high deficiency prevalence in vegetarian South Asians, warranting screening — not a different normal range.
Hyperhomocysteinemia & cobalamin (B12) deficiency in Asian Indians (Refsum et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2001)
Vitamin B12 deficiency & hyperhomocysteinemia in rural and urban Indians (Yajnik et al., JAPI 2006)
A carrier trait is not a disease; screening informs reproductive counseling and explains a spuriously altered HbA1c — it is not a diagnosis.
ACOG Practice Bulletin No. 78: Hemoglobinopathies in Pregnancy (Obstet Gynecol 2007)
ALT is not universally unreliable; the point is that a normal value is insufficient to rule out fatty liver in a high-metabolic-risk SA profile.
Proportion of NAFLD/MASLD patients with normal ALT: systematic review & meta-analysis (BMC Gastroenterol 2020)
Benign ethnic neutropenia is best-established for African ancestry; the South Asian evidence base is thinner — flag it as a possibility, not a rule.
Benign ethnic neutropenia (Atallah-Yunes et al., Blood Rev 2019)
The recommendation is genotype-guided statin selection, not SA-specific dosing; there is no SA-specific dosing table.
CPIC Guideline for SLCO1B1/ABCG2/CYP2C9 & statin-associated musculoskeletal symptoms (Clin Pharmacol Ther 2022)
These are cardiometabolic-risk-adjusted centiles on IAP 2015 charts; under-5 growth still follows WHO international standards regardless of ethnicity.
Revised IAP 2015 growth charts / BMI cut-offs for 5-18y Indian children (Khadilkar et al., Indian J Endocrinol Metab 2015)

code.text: Diabetes risk at low BMI (lean-diabetes phenotype)

extension.valueString: Lean diabetes reflects reduced β-cell function / 'thin-fat' body composition, not the absence of metabolic risk; a normal BMI is not reassurance.
Young-onset diabetes in Asian Indians linked to lower beta-cell function (INSPIRED; Diabetologia 2022)
The diagnostic thresholds are unchanged; the SA-specific action is earlier screening, not a different cutoff.
IADPSG recommendations on diagnosis & classification of hyperglycemia in pregnancy (Diabetes Care 2010)
Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2024

code.text: Age at natural menopause (South Asian women)

extension.valueString: Cohort estimate (n=405); earlier menopause raises cardiometabolic risk earlier but is not itself a disease.
Menopausal experience (hot flashes, urinary incontinence, mood) among South Asian American women — MASALA (Lyu et al., Climacteric 2025)
Association from MASALA (n~415); parity is a risk-context flag, not a screening threshold.
Association of Parity With ASCVD Risk Factors Among South Asian Women — MASALA (Mehta et al., JACC Advances 2026)
Credibility-showcase 'do-not-apply' row: the actionable target is B12 status, not MTHFR genotyping.
Hyperhomocysteinemia & cobalamin (B12) deficiency in Asian Indians (Refsum et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2001)
No SA-specific numeric ratio cutoff is defined here; the claim is the ratio's predictive weight, not a threshold.
Risk factors for early MI in South Asians (INTERHEART, JAMA 2007)
Not a redefined range — a prevalence-driven screening emphasis; do not present a different TSH normal range for SA.
Prevalence of hypothyroidism in adults: epidemiological study in eight cities of India (Unnikrishnan et al., Indian J Endocrinol Metab 2013)
